text:
TRANSTHORACIC ECHOCARDIOGRAM REPORT

PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
INDICATION: Pre-chemotherapy cardiac evaluation. New diagnosis of acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with FLT3-ITD mutation, adverse-risk per ELN 2022. Planned treatment with anthracycline-based induction chemotherapy (7+3 regimen: cytarabine + daunorubicin 60 mg/m2/day x3 days) in combination with midostaurin. Cardiac clearance requested by hematology/oncology prior to initiation of cardiotoxic therapy. History of hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus.

STUDY TYPE: Transthoracic two-dimensional echocardiogram (2D TTE) with Doppler interrogation and color flow mapping
TRANSDUCER: Phased array cardiac probe
SEDATION: None
IMAGE QUALITY: Adequate windows obtained from parasternal, apical, and subcostal approaches. Minor degradation of lateral wall endocardial border definition due to body habitus, compensated with harmonic imaging. Overall study quality: good.

---

FINDINGS:

LEFT VENTRICLE:
The left ventricular cavity is normal in size. Wall thickness is within normal limits; there is no eccentic or concentric hypertrophy, though there is mild concentric remodeling of the inferior and inferolateral walls, which may reflect chronic pressure loading in the context of known hypertension. Endocardial systolic excursion is brisk and uniform throughout all walls. Left ventricular ejection fraction is visually estimated and confirmed by modified Simpson's biplane method at 62%, well within normal range. There are no regional wall motion abnormalities in any of the 16 standard myocardial segments assessed. No ventricular aneurysm. The inferolateral wall and apex are well-visualized and contractile normally.

RIGHT VENTRICLE:
The right ventricle is normal in size and configuration. Right ventricular systolic function appears normal by visual assessment. Tricuspid annular plane systolic excursion (TAPSE) is 22 mm, consistent with normal right ventricular longitudinal function. No right ventricular wall motion abnormalities. Interventricular septum moves normally without paradox.

LEFT ATRIUM:
The left atrial size is mildly increased in the anterior-posterior dimension at 4.1 cm (borderline upper limits of normal for body surface area). Left atrial volume index was estimated at 31 mL/m2 (borderline, upper limit of normal 34 mL/m2). No intracavitary thrombus or mass identified. The left atrial appendage was not formally interrogated on this transthoracic study.

RIGHT ATRIUM:
Normal in size. No intracavitary filling defect. The interatrial septum appears intact without evidence of an atrial septal defect or patent foramen ovale by color Doppler; bubble study not performed.

MITRAL VALVE:
The mitral valve leaflets are thin and mobile with no thickening, calcification, or prolapse. Mitral leaflet coaptation is normal. Color Doppler demonstrates trace physiologic central mitral regurgitation without hemodynamic significance. No mitral stenosis. The subvalvular apparatus appears unremarkable.

DIASTOLIC FUNCTION:
Doppler assessment of mitral inflow pattern: E-wave velocity 0.74 m/s, A-wave velocity 0.65 m/s, E/A ratio 1.14, deceleration time 198 ms. Tissue Doppler imaging at the medial mitral annulus: e' 9.2 cm/s; lateral annulus e' 12.1 cm/s; average septal-lateral e' 10.7 cm/s. E/e' ratio 6.9 (well below threshold of concern at 14). Overall pattern is consistent with normal diastolic function (Grade I, normal relaxation). No elevated filling pressures.

AORTIC VALVE:
The aortic valve is trileaflet with thin, pliable leaflets and no evidence of calcification or thickening. Aortic valve opening is brisk. Color Doppler shows no aortic regurgitation. Continuous-wave Doppler across the aortic valve: peak gradient 8 mmHg, mean gradient 4 mmHg (normal, no stenosis). Aortic valve area not formally calculated given no hemodynamic concern.

TRICUSPID VALVE:
The tricuspid valve is morphologically normal. Trace tricuspid regurgitation is present by color Doppler; this is physiologic. The tricuspid regurgitation jet was insufficient for reliable estimation of pulmonary artery systolic pressure; no elevation in right heart pressures is suspected based on the normal right ventricular size and function.

PULMONIC VALVE:
The pulmonic valve is not well-visualized on this study, however the right ventricular outflow tract is unobstructed by color Doppler interrogation. No pulmonic regurgitation of significance detected.

AORTA AND GREAT VESSELS:
The aortic root diameter is 3.2 cm (normal). The sinotubular junction and proximal ascending aorta appear normal in caliber. No aneurysmal dilation. The descending thoracic aorta, as seen from parasternal long-axis and suprasternal views, appears unremarkable.

PERICARDIUM:
No pericardial effusion. The pericardium appears thin and echogenic without evidence of constriction or thickening.

IVC/HEPATIC VEINS:
The inferior vena cava diameter is 1.6 cm with normal inspiratory collapse (>50%), consistent with normal central venous pressure (estimated 3-5 mmHg). No Doppler evidence of elevated right atrial pressure.

SUMMARY OF MEASUREMENTS:
  - LV end-diastolic diameter (parasternal):      4.9 cm (normal)
  - LV end-systolic diameter:                     3.3 cm (normal)
  - Interventricular septal thickness (diastole): 1.0 cm (normal)
  - Posterior wall thickness (diastole):          0.9 cm (normal)
  - LV ejection fraction (biplane Simpson):       62% (normal, lower reference limit 52%)
  - Left atrial AP diameter:                      4.1 cm (borderline)
  - Left atrial volume index:                     31 mL/m2 (borderline normal)
  - TAPSE:                                        22 mm (normal)
  - E/A ratio:                                    1.14 (normal)
  - E/e' (average):                               6.9 (normal)
  - Aortic root:                                  3.2 cm (normal)
  - Peak aortic gradient:                         8 mmHg (normal)

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Normal left ventricular size and systolic function. Ejection fraction of 62% by biplane Simpson's method. No wall motion abnormalities. No structural abnormality that would preclude anthracycline-based chemotherapy.

2. Normal diastolic function. No echocardiographic evidence of elevated left ventricular filling pressures.

3. Mildly increased left atrial size (borderline), likely related to long-standing hypertension, without hemodynamic significance at this time.

4. Mild concentric left ventricular remodeling without frank hypertrophy, consistent with hypertensive heart disease. No adverse impact on current systolic function.

5. No valvular disease of hemodynamic significance. Trace mitral and tricuspid regurgitation - physiologic.

6. No pericardial effusion.

7. Normal right ventricular size and function.

Overall, cardiac function is adequate for planned anthracycline-based induction chemotherapy. Baseline ejection fraction documented at 62% for future comparison. Close cardiac surveillance is recommended given cumulative anthracycline exposure anticipated in this patient's treatment course.

Electronic signature: Attending Cardiologist, Non-Invasive Cardiology
